title: Legal / formal prose
description: Contract-style language — long sentences, formal tone
text: Subject to the terms and conditions set forth in this Agreement, the Provider hereby grants to the Customer a non-exclusive, non-transferable, revocable license to access and use the Service solely for internal business purposes, during the term of this Agreement and in accordance with the documentation provided.

tips.text: Contract prose runs long on purpose. The sentences stretch, the clauses pile up, and you keep an even hand all the way through.
Treat a word like "hereinafter" as one block, not nine letters. Stamp it whole and the long sentence stops being long.

tips.target: Subject to the terms and conditions set forth in this Agreement, the Provider hereby grants to the Customer a non-exclusive, non-transferable, revocable license

tips.text: Lawyers and paralegals type this all day, and a single dropped word can cost a clause its meaning. Accuracy is respect here, and nowhere more so.

tips.target: to access and use the Service solely for internal business purposes, during the term of this Agreement and in accordance with the documentation provided.

character_tips.anna: Legal English is formal, but it has a rhythm under the weight. Find it and the long sentences carry you along.
character_tips.maxim: Hold 85 on a contract and you are well ahead of most who do this for a living. Precision is the whole point.
character_tips.knopych: Long sentences, no fear. Keep your eyes up and let the clauses unspool.
character_tips.klavochka: Type it the way a proper document deserves: careful, even, and clean to the last full stop. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
